steps.title: Identify what the blank must convey
steps.content: The passage says Polynesian languages have a certain kind of consonant array, then gives examples of 8 and 11 consonants, and then notes the global median is over 22. The contrast between the Polynesian examples and the global median is the key signal: the blank must describe an array that is small or limited relative to other languages.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a musical': means relating to music, not to size or quantity — this does not match the contrast the passage draws between Polynesian consonant counts and the global median. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an unvarying': means unchanging or uniform, but the passage says nothing about consistency within Polynesian languages — this introduces an idea not supported by the text. ✗
  • Choice C — 'an exclusive': means limited to a select group, not small in number — this does not fit the comparison of consonant counts. ✗
  • Choice D — 'a modest': means relatively small or limited in size, which directly matches the contrast: 8–11 consonants versus a global median of over 22. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
